What You'll Learn

8 Product Categories

Rod Wipers

Remove contaminants from cylinder rods before they enter the gland. Understand lip designs, heel types, and material variants.

↗ 24 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Rod Seals

Primary and buffer seals for hydraulic and pneumatic cylinders. U-cups, buffer rings, anti-extrusion designs.

↗ 30 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Symmetrical Seals

PolyPak® and U-cup seals usable on rod or piston. Spring-loaded, beveled, and modular backup variants.

↗ 14 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Piston Seals

Single and double-acting piston seals. PTFE, nitrile, polyurethane, and cast iron variants for all pressure classes.

↗ 38 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Vee Packings

Multi-ring vee packing sets for high-pressure rod and piston applications. Inch and metric configurations.

↗ 6 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Guiding Elements

Wear rings, bearing strips, and DU bearings to prevent metal-to-metal contact and keep parts centered.

↗ 18 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

O-Rings & Accessories

Standard, metric, specialty O-rings, back-up rings, bonded seals, kits and accessories from Parker and more.

↗ 24 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗

Misc Accessories

Caps & plugs, anti-seize compounds, lock nuts, and retaining rings.

↗ 4 Series Shop on sealsonline.com ↗
Category A

Rod Wipers

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
24 series · Inch & Metric
⚠

Key Training Point

Always identify whether the heel is solid or slotted — slotted heels prevent pressure traps between wiper and rod seal. Check lip count (single vs double) before ordering.

Category B

Rod Seals

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
30 series · Inch & Metric
📐

Measurement

Most U-cup rod seals measure c.s. / i.d. / o.d. / hgt. Buffer seals measure i.d., o.d., and overall hgt. CAT-specific seals use housing dimensions — confirm whether listed dimension is seal or housing.

Category C

Symmetrical Seals

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
14 series · Inch & Metric
🔄

Training Note

Symmetrical seals work on both rod and piston. PolyPak® family measures cs / i.d. / o.d. / hgt. Last 4 digits in Type B part numbers identify the material.

Category D

Piston Seals

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
38 series · Inch & Metric
📏

Key Training Point

Piston seals measure o.d. / i.d. / hgt. — OD listed first. PTFE piston rings are solid outer rings loaded with a square tetra seal expander. Iron rings come in many cut types — confirm cut before ordering.

Category E/M

Vee Packings

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
6 series · Inch & Metric
Category F/N

Guiding Elements & Wear Rings

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
18 series · Inch & Metric
⚙

DU Bearing Note

When measuring DU bearings, never scratch the PTFE inner layer with caliper points. Measure ID, OD, and height only when bearing is fully closed.

Category G/O

O-Rings & Accessories

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
24 series · Inch & Metric
🔍

Verification Checklist

For every O-ring always verify: i.d., cross section, material, and durometer. For kits, also check box label and quantity. For cord styles, verify the correct profile (O-ring, quad, or square).

Category H

Miscellaneous Accessories

Browse all on sealsonline.com ↗
4 series
Reference Guide

Measurement Abbreviations

Every product entry uses these standard abbreviations. Know them before you pick up a caliper.

Related Training
Dial Caliper Guide
How to read & zero the Mitutoyo 505
↗
i.d.
Inner Diameter
The bore or rod dimension the seal contacts internally.
o.d.
Outer Diameter
The housing or bore dimension the seal contacts externally.
c.s.
Cross Section
Width of the seal ring itself, measured radially.
hgt.
Height
Axial length of the seal from face to face.
T
Thickness
Used for back-up rings; same as cross section.
1st hgt.
First / Base Height
The shorter face on multi-height wipers or seals.
2nd hgt.
Second / Overall Height
The taller face or total assembly height.
duro.
Durometer (Shore)
Hardness rating; most seals are 70A–95A. Always check.

Wipers — what to measure

Identify: i.d., o.d., base height (1st hgt.), and whether the heel is open or closed. Note lip count. For steel-cased types, also confirm the canning height.

U-cup rod seals

Measure: c.s., i.d., o.d., and hgt. For BS-style seals: i.d. + 2 c.s. = o.d. is a useful check. Unloaded seals are measured without a spring.

Piston seals — OD first

OD is always listed first in piston seal part numbers. Measure o.d., i.d., and hgt. For multi-piece designs, note each component height separately.

PTFE solid outer rings

Measure the outer ring: o.d., i.d., and hgt./cs. The expander size is listed separately in catalog. Never crush or overtighten during measurement.

Materials Reference

Common Seal Materials

Material selection affects compatibility, temperature range, and fluid resistance. Always confirm with the customer's application data.

Polyurethane
AU 90A · AU 92A · AU 93A · AU 94A
High abrasion resistanceExcellent wear lifeGood oil resistance

The most common seal elastomer at Fluidseal. Different durometer grades control hardness and sealing force. 94A is harder — used for higher-pressure buffer applications.

Nitrile
NBR · 70A · 80A · 80/90A · 90A
Petroleum fluid compatibleWide temperature rangeCost-effective

Standard elastomer for petroleum-based hydraulic fluids. Carboxylated Nitrile (XNBR) adds improved abrasion resistance.

PTFE
BR.PTFE · GF.PTFE · V.PTFE
Very low frictionChemical resistantWide temp range

Bronze-filled (BR.PTFE) and Glass-filled (GF.PTFE) grades add strength and wear resistance. Used in high-performance piston and rod seals where low friction is critical.

Fluorocarbon
FKM · Viton®
High temperatureChemical resistantAggressive fluids

Used where nitrile is incompatible — high temperatures, aggressive chemicals, phosphate ester fluids. Confirm application before substituting.

Nylon / Polyamide
PA · GF.Nylon
Anti-extrusionWear ringsStructural

Glass-filled nylon used for wear rings and anti-extrusion rings. Common in multi-piece piston seals (TP, P2G, CTS, P4A).

Neo-Fabric
Neoprene fabric
FlexibleSplit-to-diameterVee packings

Fabric-reinforced neoprene. Primary material for vee packing center rings and certain piston seals (CF, P3C). Can be split to almost any diameter for vee coil applications.

Parker Material Selection Guide

Periodic Table of Elastomers

Click any element for temperature range, fluid resistance, and Parker compound codes. Source: Parker ORD 5712.
